slides.title: It's not your step count. It's your *pace*.
slides.sub: How fast you walk may predict your years better than how many steps you log. Most trackers miss it.

slides.title: Speed tracks with lifespan
slides.body: In large studies, a *brisker walking pace* lines up with lower mortality, beyond total steps alone.

slides.title: Aim for a brisk clip
slides.body: Brisk means roughly *100+ steps a minute* — you can talk, but not easily sing.

slides.title: Pace stresses the engine
slides.body: Walking faster pushes your *heart and lungs* a little harder, which is what drives the fitness payoff.

slides.title: Pick up the last block
slides.body: Keep your usual walk, just *finish faster*. Short bursts of pace add up without extra time.

slides.title: Same minutes, more return
slides.body: You're already out there. *Adding intensity* gives you more health per minute walked.

slides.title: Make every walk *count for more*.
slides.body: Vibe Health folds your steps and pace into one daily score — so you can see your walks getting brisker and your fitness trending up.

caption:
Everyone chases 10k steps. The research says your pace might matter more. 🚶

A brisk clip — roughly 100+ steps a minute — lines up with lower mortality beyond step count. Easiest tweak: just finish your usual walk faster. 🔖

Vibe Health folds your steps and pace into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Walking for Longevity: Pace Beats Step Count

bg_prompts.cover: Black-and-white cinematic editorial photograph. Dramatic low-key lighting, deep shadows, dark charcoal background. Realistic texture, shallow depth of field. Single subject, calm confident mood. No text, no logos. Vertical composition with the subject toward the upper half so the lower third falls into darkness. A person walking briskly down an empty city sidewalk at dusk, motion in the stride, low light raking across the scene.
